_meta.provenance: Module-specific Layer-1 integration register: how each module relates to each existing system. Every integration is a Proposed design; system facts come from the verified systems register. Modules not listed here have not been authored yet (Phase 2 in progress, pillar by pillar).

integrations.why: Academies and training centres registered on NSRS map to directory nodes, so the org tree and the registry describe the same institutions.
integrations.dataFlow: NSRS institution records → directory node seeding and cross-checks (one-way, periodic).
integrations.kbValueAdd: NSRS lists institutions; the directory adds hierarchy, roles, jurisdictions and routing.
integrations.gaps: NSRS does not model administrative hierarchy, sanctioned posts or jurisdictions.

integrations.why: eOffice instances already encode ministry/department role hierarchies for file routing.
integrations.dataFlow: eOffice organisational units → cross-check of directory nodes (no live sync proposed initially).
integrations.kbValueAdd: One public, sports-wide directory across bodies that live in separate eOffice instances.
integrations.gaps: eOffice hierarchies are per-instance and not publicly queryable.

integrations.why: Hosting prerequisite for the first platform registry.
integrations.dataFlow: None — infrastructure requirement.

integrations.why: The NSGA 2025 is the statutory basis: recognition, elections, tenure/cooling-off, athlete commissions, Tribunal — M2 is its digital compliance backbone.
integrations.dataFlow: Statute → compliance rule set encoded in the registry (recognition states, filing obligations).
integrations.kbValueAdd: Turns a paper statute into a live, queryable compliance scorecard.
integrations.gaps: NSB operating procedures and register formats not yet published; select provisions only in force since 31 Dec 2025.

integrations.why: NSRS registers federations/academies; recognition status must attach to the same entities.
integrations.dataFlow: NSRS federation records ↔ registry entity matching (shared identifiers needed).
integrations.kbValueAdd: Recognition + compliance status on top of bare registration.
integrations.gaps: No public federation-status API on NSRS.

integrations.why: PFMS is the authoritative source for sanction → release → utilisation of central scheme money.
integrations.dataFlow: PFMS payment-stage events for sports schemes → application timeline mirror (read-only).
integrations.kbValueAdd: Athlete-visible fund tracking attached to the application, not buried in a finance portal.
integrations.gaps: No confirmed external API; state-funded grants flow outside PFMS (Q3).

integrations.why: Scheme files physically move through eOffice; applicants should see desk and days-pending.
integrations.dataFlow: eOffice file-status events (file no. ↔ application ID) → status mirror (read-only).
integrations.kbValueAdd: “Where is my file” answered without RTI; delay analytics by desk.
integrations.gaps: No public eOffice API; mapping file numbers to applications needs ministry cooperation.

integrations.why: DigiLocker is the confirmed-API path for document submission and later certificate issuance.
integrations.dataFlow: Issued documents pulled with applicant consent; KB-issued certificates pushed as issuer (later).
integrations.kbValueAdd: Zero photocopies; forgery-resistant documents.
integrations.gaps: Requires formal partner onboarding (SOP) — not yet applied for.

integrations.why: Khelo India scholarships/TOPS stipends are the highest-volume schemes to onboard first.
integrations.dataFlow: Scheme masters + beneficiary decisions ↔ application workflow.
integrations.kbValueAdd: One application front door; eligibility engine over scheme rules.
integrations.gaps: Scheme rules exist as documents, not structured eligibility rules.

integrations.why: Benefit payouts ride DBT rails; status closes the loop for the applicant.
integrations.dataFlow: DBT payment confirmations → application timeline (read-only).
integrations.kbValueAdd: “Money reached the account” visible next to the approval.
integrations.gaps: DBT confirmation is via PFMS; direct confirmation pending (S12 partially verified).

integrations.why: CPGRAMS is the national grievance system connected to 92 ministries and 36 states/UTs — a sports channel must interoperate, not compete.
integrations.dataFlow: Sports cases raised in KB get CPGRAMS cross-references where applicable; category mapping agreed with DARPG.
integrations.kbValueAdd: Sport-specific routing (via M1 jurisdictions), SLA clocks and validity filtering in front of the generic national system.
integrations.gaps: Whether DARPG permits a domain front-end or requires cases to live wholly in CPGRAMS is undecided (Q19).

integrations.why: DPDP applies to targeted messaging and grievance records (minors' safeguarding cases especially).
integrations.dataFlow: None — compliance constraint shaping consent and retention design.
integrations.gaps: Final DPDP Rules phase-in affects messaging-consent design.

integrations.why: Schools already run annual fitness assessments on Fit India's age-appropriate GOALS protocols — the screening games must extend this battery, not compete with it.
integrations.dataFlow: Fit India protocol definitions → screening test battery; school registration status ← cross-checked.
integrations.kbValueAdd: Turns a fitness-rating exercise into a talent-discovery funnel with verified results.
integrations.gaps: Fit India assesses fitness but does not shortlist or route talent anywhere.

integrations.why: UDISE+ gives every recognised school an 11-digit code — the stable identifier the screening network keys on.
integrations.dataFlow: UDISE+ school records → school register seeding and yearly refresh (read-only).
integrations.kbValueAdd: No new school registry; screening coverage is measurable against the real national school universe.
integrations.gaps: UDISE+ has no sports data, facilities detail beyond basics, or screening history.

integrations.why: KIRTI's Talent Assessment Centres are the next step for children who stand out in school screening.
integrations.dataFlow: Top-percentile shortlists → KIRTI referral queue; KIRTI assessment outcomes → child's timeline.
integrations.kbValueAdd: KIRTI gets a pre-filtered, geography-complete funnel instead of self-selected registrations.
integrations.gaps: KIRTI Phase 1 assessed ~51,000 of a 20-lakh target — the funnel, not the centres, is the bottleneck.

integrations.why: APAAR is the student's lifetime education ID; linking it (with guardian consent) connects screening history to the child's education record.
integrations.dataFlow: APAAR ID ← guardian-consented linkage at enrolment; no education data pulled without purpose.
integrations.kbValueAdd: Screening results survive school transfers; dedupe across schools becomes trivial.
integrations.gaps: APAAR consent is school-mediated and withdrawable — linkage must handle revocation.

integrations.why: A screened child who continues in sport needs one identity from day one — the Khelo India ID issued by NSRS.
integrations.dataFlow: Standout athletes get NSRS registration initiated (guardian-consented); KID attaches to screening history.
integrations.kbValueAdd: The 'first scouted by' record and the elite career share one identity spine.
integrations.gaps: NSRS self-registration assumes motivated adults; child-onboarding flow does not exist publicly.

integrations.why: Teacher–school mapping rides the same UDISE+ school codes; a scout's jurisdiction is their school.
integrations.dataFlow: School codes → scout registration validation (teacher claims verified against school).
integrations.kbValueAdd: A national scout force addressable by real school geography.
integrations.gaps: UDISE+ teacher data is administrative — scout enrolment still needs education-department consent.

integrations.why: Fit India school registration and ratings already engage PT teachers — the scout programme is the recognition layer on the same network.
integrations.dataFlow: Fit India school participation status → scout-programme eligibility signals.
integrations.kbValueAdd: Teachers get named, credited and rewarded for discovery work Fit India already implies.
integrations.gaps: Fit India recognises schools, not individual teachers.

integrations.why: NSRS registers coaches and support personnel; scouts belong on the same registry so discovery credit is portable.
integrations.dataFlow: Scout registrations → NSRS support-personnel records (or linked profile).
integrations.kbValueAdd: 'First scouted by' becomes a permanent, verifiable career credit.
integrations.gaps: No public scout/talent-spotter category confirmed on NSRS.

integrations.why: NSRS is the proposed identity backbone: every record on the 360 timeline attaches to the Khelo India ID (KID).
integrations.dataFlow: Identity lookup/create ↔ NSRS; profile events (results, assessments, benefits) attach to KID.
integrations.kbValueAdd: NSRS holds registrations; the 360 profile assembles the verified career timeline across all systems.
integrations.gaps: No public API confirmed (Q1); 'NSRS ID' vs KID terminology unresolved (Q13).

integrations.why: KIRTI assessments are the highest-signal standardised talent data points for school-age athletes.
integrations.dataFlow: KIRTI assessment results → 360 timeline entries (verified · SAI centre).
integrations.kbValueAdd: Assessment results stop living in a programme silo and start following the athlete.
integrations.gaps: KIRTI-to-NSRS identity linkage not publicly documented.

integrations.why: Aadhaar-backed verification (via DigiLocker) and APAAR linkage are the dedupe anchors — one person, one profile.
integrations.dataFlow: eKYC verification events (yes/no + minimal fields) — raw Aadhaar numbers never stored.
integrations.kbValueAdd: Duplicate and ghost profiles become detectable at registration time.
integrations.gaps: Aadhaar usage rules for a sports platform need legal confirmation.

integrations.why: Selection committees need to know an athlete's doping status — but testing data lives in WADA's ADAMS under strict rules.
integrations.dataFlow: Status flag only (eligible / sanctioned, with sanction window) — never test details.
integrations.kbValueAdd: Selectors stop discovering sanctions from newspapers.
integrations.gaps: NADA/WADA data-sharing constraints are stricter than any other integration here.

integrations.why: Khelo India Games results and TOPS/NCOE participation are core verified timeline events.
integrations.dataFlow: Games results + programme participation → 360 timeline (verified · source programme).
integrations.kbValueAdd: A single career view across programmes that currently do not share screens.
integrations.gaps: Games Management System (GMS) integration path unconfirmed (Q29).

integrations.why: Fit India assessment history gives the 360 profile its grassroots fitness baseline.
integrations.dataFlow: School-age fitness assessments → baseline metrics on the timeline (consent-gated).
integrations.kbValueAdd: Talent signals visible years before formal competition results exist.
integrations.gaps: Fit India app data linkage to a sports profile is not an existing feature.

integrations.why: SAI already operates a Games Management System (GMS) for Khelo India Games — M8 must coordinate with it, not duplicate it.
integrations.dataFlow: Sanctioned-event calendar ↔ GMS; Khelo India Games results → verified results feed.
integrations.kbValueAdd: One national calendar and one results standard across events GMS does not cover (school, district, federation).
integrations.gaps: GMS scope, availability to non-Khelo-India events, and integration path unconfirmed (Q29).

integrations.why: Event entries keyed to the athlete's KID make every start list verifiable and every result attributable.
integrations.dataFlow: Entry validation ← NSRS identity; participation + results → athlete 360 timeline.
integrations.kbValueAdd: No more paper entry forms with unverifiable identities; age fraud gets harder.
integrations.gaps: Depends on the same unconfirmed NSRS API as M7.

integrations.why: Participation and medal certificates issued straight to DigiLocker are forgery-resistant and instantly verifiable.
integrations.dataFlow: KB as DigiLocker issuer → certificates pushed to participants' lockers.
integrations.kbValueAdd: Certificates that scheme applications (M3) and marks-for-sports (M16) can trust without manual verification.
integrations.gaps: Requires issuer onboarding — same SOP as M3's requester role.

integrations.why: NSRS already registers coaches and support personnel — the registry must extend those records, not duplicate them.
integrations.dataFlow: NSRS coach/support records ↔ registry profiles (shared identity).
integrations.kbValueAdd: Adds qualifications, deployment history, officiating panels and vetting state on top of bare registration.
integrations.gaps: No public API (Q1); qualification fields on NSRS not publicly documented.

integrations.why: The NSGA 2025 framework makes federations accountable for officiating standards — the registry gives that accountability a roster to point at.
integrations.dataFlow: Statutory context → panel structures per sport.
integrations.kbValueAdd: Officials' appointments become traceable to recognised bodies.
integrations.gaps: No statutory officials register exists yet.

integrations.why: Coaching diplomas and degrees (NIS, LNIPE, NSU) verified via DigiLocker issued documents kill credential fraud.
integrations.dataFlow: Issued qualification documents pulled with consent at registration.
integrations.kbValueAdd: A 'verified credential' badge that academies and parents can trust.
integrations.gaps: Not all sports institutions issue to DigiLocker yet.

integrations.why: PM GatiShakti is the national GIS with 1,450+ layers — the right long-term home for a sports-facility layer.
integrations.dataFlow: Facility registry → proposed sports layer on the NMP platform; base layers ← for planning views.
integrations.kbValueAdd: Sports infrastructure becomes visible in national infrastructure planning.
integrations.gaps: No sports layer exists today; DPIIT/BISAG-N engagement not started (Q33).

integrations.why: UDISE+ records school infrastructure including playgrounds — the largest single inventory of grassroots sports space.
integrations.dataFlow: School playground fields → facility registry (school-ground category).
integrations.kbValueAdd: Grassroots access mapping, not just stadium mapping.
integrations.gaps: UDISE+ playground data is coarse (yes/no, not condition).

integrations.why: Khelo India funds infrastructure projects — those belong on the map with their funding trail.
integrations.dataFlow: Khelo India infrastructure project list → facility records with scheme linkage.
integrations.kbValueAdd: Funded-vs-built visibility per district.
integrations.gaps: Project data lives in scheme files, not a queryable registry.

integrations.why: Assets bought on GeM carry clean purchase metadata — capturing it at source means the register starts complete.
integrations.dataFlow: GeM order metadata (sports buyers) → asset-register intake queue.
integrations.kbValueAdd: No retro data entry for new purchases; price benchmarks per item emerge free.
integrations.gaps: GeM feed availability for a domain platform unconfirmed (S19 partially verified).

integrations.why: Scheme-funded equipment (Khelo India grants) must appear as trackable assets, closing the grant→asset loop.
integrations.dataFlow: Approved equipment grants (M3) → expected-asset records awaiting receipt confirmation.
integrations.kbValueAdd: 'Money left, equipment arrived' verification — the anti-leakage view.
integrations.gaps: Requires M3 scheme onboarding first.

integrations.why: Utilisation certificates for equipment grants reconcile against PFMS releases.
integrations.dataFlow: PFMS release refs ↔ asset receipt confirmations (reporting view).
integrations.kbValueAdd: Utilisation reporting grounded in physical assets, not paper certificates.
integrations.gaps: Same PFMS access dependency as M3.

integrations.why: Academies self-register on NSRS (verified fact) — accreditation attaches to those same entities.
integrations.dataFlow: NSRS academy records ↔ accreditation register (shared identity).
integrations.kbValueAdd: Registration becomes the entry ramp; accreditation the quality signal.
integrations.gaps: NSRS academy data fields not publicly documented.

integrations.why: Khelo India already runs academy accreditation — M12 must extend the existing programme, not invent a rival standard.
integrations.dataFlow: Existing accredited-academy list → register seed; new assessments follow the agreed standard.
integrations.kbValueAdd: One public, searchable accreditation state with inspection history.
integrations.gaps: Current accreditation standards and coverage need confirmation (Q37).

integrations.why: Accreditation certificates issued to DigiLocker are tamper-proof marketing assets for academies.
integrations.dataFlow: Platform as issuer → accreditation certificates to academy lockers.
integrations.kbValueAdd: Parents can verify an academy's claim in seconds.
integrations.gaps: Issuer onboarding shared with M8.

integrations.why: ABDM's consent-manager architecture is the lawful way to handle athlete medical records — better than building a parallel store.
integrations.dataFlow: Injury/treatment records exchanged via ABHA-linked consent; platform holds care-pathway state, not raw records.
integrations.kbValueAdd: Athletes keep one health record; sports care plugs into it.
integrations.gaps: ABDM integration path for a sports platform unexplored (Q39); S20 partially verified.

integrations.why: Anti-doping safety in treatment: medication checks against the prohibited list and TUE guidance belong in the injury workflow.
integrations.dataFlow: Reference content and referral links — no ADAMS data flows here.
integrations.kbValueAdd: A treating physio can check a prescription's doping risk in-flow.
integrations.gaps: Content-reuse permission needed.

integrations.why: Injury and availability status must attach to the same athlete identity as everything else.
integrations.dataFlow: KID ↔ care episodes (minimal-disclosure availability flag to M7).
integrations.kbValueAdd: Selectors see 'available/rehab' without seeing diagnoses.
integrations.gaps: Same NSRS dependency as all identity modules.

integrations.why: The card IS a co-branded RuPay product — NPCI and an issuing bank are the delivery mechanism (verified precedent in S11 facts).
integrations.dataFlow: Tier entitlement state → issuing bank; card lifecycle events ← bank (never transaction data).
integrations.kbValueAdd: Government benefits ride existing payment infrastructure instead of a new card scheme.
integrations.gaps: A government-athlete co-brand has no direct precedent; bank selection process undefined.

integrations.why: Card tiers derive from the verified athlete profile — no verified identity, no card.
integrations.dataFlow: Verified achievement level (M7) → tier eligibility computation.
integrations.kbValueAdd: The card becomes the physical token of the digital identity — the adoption engine.
integrations.gaps: Depends on A-M7-1 and the verification chain.

integrations.why: Scholarships already flow via DBT — landing them in the card-linked account closes the benefits loop.
integrations.dataFlow: DBT credit confirmations → cardholder benefit timeline.
integrations.kbValueAdd: One place where the athlete sees money, entitlements and usage.
integrations.gaps: Shares S12's partially-verified status.

integrations.why: Benefit-programme spend reporting reconciles against PFMS releases.
integrations.dataFlow: Programme spend aggregates ← PFMS references.
integrations.kbValueAdd: The Benefits layer stays auditable.
integrations.gaps: Same PFMS access dependency.

integrations.why: Sports-quota recruitment runs on claimed sports careers — the registry makes those claims verifiable in one lookup.
integrations.dataFlow: Consented credential snapshot (verified results, laurels) → employer application view.
integrations.kbValueAdd: Kills the attested-photocopy economy in sports-quota recruitment.
integrations.gaps: Employers must formally accept digital verification (Q43).

integrations.why: NCS is the national job-matching rail — sports vacancies should flow through it, not around it.
integrations.dataFlow: Sports-sector vacancies ↔ NCS listings; athlete profiles → NCS candidate pool (consented).
integrations.kbValueAdd: Athletes reach the whole employment ecosystem, not just sports quotas.
integrations.gaps: S21 partially verified; integration path unexplored.

integrations.why: Recruitment document packs (certificates, IDs) assemble from DigiLocker instead of couriered attestations.
integrations.dataFlow: Issued documents shared to employers with candidate consent.
integrations.kbValueAdd: Verification effort per candidate drops from weeks to seconds.
integrations.gaps: Employer-side acceptance workflows needed.

integrations.why: NCrF explicitly creditises sports (verified) — M16 operationalises that mapping for verified achievements.
integrations.dataFlow: Achievement level (verified chain) → NCrF credit mapping → board/university award.
integrations.kbValueAdd: Turns a policy possibility into an administrable process with evidence standards.
integrations.gaps: Credit-point mapping for sports levels not yet defined by any board (Q9, Q45).

integrations.why: Credits must land in the student's Academic Bank of Credits via APAAR to count.
integrations.dataFlow: Awarded sports credits → ABC via APAAR linkage (consented).
integrations.kbValueAdd: Sports achievement becomes part of the permanent academic record.
integrations.gaps: ABC write-path for non-academic issuers unconfirmed.

integrations.why: The evidence for a credit claim is a DigiLocker-verifiable certificate from M8.
integrations.dataFlow: Event certificates (D-M8-5) → credit-claim evidence.
integrations.kbValueAdd: Boards verify claims without paper committees.
integrations.gaps: Requires M8 issuer path live.

integrations.why: Credit-worthy results must come from events sanctioned by recognised bodies — the NSGA recognition chain is the fraud firewall.
integrations.dataFlow: Recognition state (M2) gates which results qualify.
integrations.kbValueAdd: Marks-for-sports without a fake-tournament industry.
integrations.gaps: Depends on M2 and A-M8-1 being live first.

integrations.why: CSR sports funding is lawful under Schedule VII — the exchange must map needs to those categories and reconcile with CSR disclosures.
integrations.dataFlow: Need categories → Schedule VII mapping; funded-project records → disclosure references.
integrations.kbValueAdd: Corporates get audit-ready CSR documentation; government sees where CSR actually lands.
integrations.gaps: S22 partially verified; MCA guidance on platform-mediated CSR unexplored (Q47).

integrations.why: Where CSR co-funds government projects, the public-money side reconciles via PFMS.
integrations.dataFlow: Co-funded project references ← PFMS (read-only).
integrations.kbValueAdd: Blended-funding projects stay transparent on both sides.
integrations.gaps: Same PFMS dependency.

integrations.why: Every story and laurel anchors to a verified identity and verified achievements — no fabricated heroes.
integrations.dataFlow: Verified profile + laurels (M7) → consented showcase content.
integrations.kbValueAdd: Media trust: every claim on a profile traces to a verifier.
integrations.gaps: Consent framework for minors in media (Q49).

integrations.why: Laurels backed by DigiLocker-verifiable certificates are publishable without manual checks.
integrations.dataFlow: Certificate references → laurel verification state.
integrations.kbValueAdd: The Laurels wall is self-verifying.
integrations.gaps: Requires M8 issuance live.

integrations.why: Spend-vs-outcome analytics need the money side — PFMS aggregates per scheme and geography.
integrations.dataFlow: PFMS aggregates → command-centre spend dashboards.
integrations.kbValueAdd: 'What did the money produce' becomes answerable per district.
integrations.gaps: Aggregation level PFMS will share is undefined.

integrations.why: Infrastructure analytics overlay the facility layer on GatiShakti base layers (population, connectivity).
integrations.dataFlow: Facility + utilisation aggregates ↔ NMP planning layers.
integrations.kbValueAdd: Gap maps: where talent density meets facility deserts.
integrations.gaps: Depends on A-M10-1 engagement.

integrations.why: Anonymised aggregate datasets belong on the national open-data platform under NDSAP.
integrations.dataFlow: Approved anonymised extracts → data.gov.in publication.
integrations.kbValueAdd: Researchers and startups build on sports data without bespoke agreements.
integrations.gaps: S23 partially verified; publication approval path pending.

integrations.why: DPDP bounds everything M20 does — anonymisation standards, purpose limitation, and children's-data prohibitions.
integrations.dataFlow: None — governing constraint on every extract.
integrations.gaps: Re-identification risk standard undefined (Q53).
